lexical_units.contextual_meaning: collective resolve or service is framed through body parts, soul, or embodied strength
lexical_units.basic_meaning: literal organs, limbs, inner life, or bodily strength
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The phrase applies bodily or inner-life language to collective political commitment.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: National service is understood through embodied agency.
lexical_units.review_notes: Some uses are idiomatic; keep confidence moderate.

lexical_units.contextual_meaning: wartime service or loss is framed as sacrifice for freedom, country, or allies
lexical_units.basic_meaning: religious or material offering, loss, or giving up something valuable
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The passage concerns civic and military cost rather than a literal ritual offering.
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Wartime obligation is understood through sacrificial offering.
lexical_units.review_notes: High-salience expansion term; keep provisional pending independent review.
